_schema.purpose: Editorial cost-structure assumptions feeding the Sankey balancing engine (wq-055). Used as estimation fallback when entity-level data isn't sourced/derivable. Hand-curated; provenance documented per parameter.
_schema.principle: sourced > derived > estimated — engine prefers entity-documented data; falls back here only when entity data is absent

inference_estimation._doc: TIER 3 fallback for resolve_inference_cost(). Used only when entity has no inference_cost field AND no derivable opex+op_loss path. Per-provider rates; _default for unmapped providers.

providerRates.openai.src: Frontier model providers run inference-heavy; 0.55× revenue conservative

providerRates.google.src: Google's TPU-based inference cheaper per token than GPU-based

providerRates.iaas.src: IaaS providers (Together/Fireworks/Groq) are pass-through; lower inference rate

providerRates._default.src: Catch-all for new providers without specific rate; midpoint of frontier and efficient

opex_estimation._doc: TIER 3 fallback for resolve_opex(). Size-band rates capture the typical opex-to-revenue pattern: early-stage opex outpaces revenue, mature operations achieve efficiency at scale.

size_bands.src: Editorial estimate of opex-to-revenue ratio scaled by company size band; reflects typical pattern of opex efficiency improving with scale. Not derived from data — calibrate against known cases. Phase A confirmed sane for 2025 model_provider population.

marginPcts._doc: Channel pass-through margins. Hyperscalers take 20% on AI model serving; Trad SaaS takes 60% on AI SKUs. Editorial; not derived.

Hyperscalers.src: Industry-standard hyperscaler take rate on AI model serving (~20%)

Trad. SaaS.src: Industry-standard SaaS gross margin (~60%) for AI add-on SKUs

channelRouting._doc: Editorial channel structure. wq-055 used proportional rescaling of channel TOTALS only. wq-062 introduces per-provider routing via channel_mapping below — engine reads each entity's revenue_by_channel and writes an explicit per-provider-per-channel routing block. Renderer reads routing block; falls back to proportional rescale if routing is absent.

channel_mapping._comment: wq-090 §3 — per-archetype channel routing replaces the wq-062 flat 70/30 api_pct → Model API/Hyperscalers split. Each provider entity carries an `entity_archetype` field (frontier_lab / ai_native / enterprise_saas / hyperscaler / iaas / consumer_app); the engine looks up the matching weights below and applies them to the entity's revenue_by_channel slice. Falls back to `_default` when the archetype is missing or unknown — under-counting Hyperscalers is editorially safer than over-counting (the bug wq-090 corrects). Channel margins (Hyperscalers 0.20, Trad SaaS 0.60) still gross up channel.value so chPass = sum(provider routing per channel).

buyer_provider_split._doc: wq-090 editorial — per-buyer-segment provider preference (net basis). AI-Native cohort customers (Cursor / Glean / Perplexity / Harvey / etc.) consume frontier APIs roughly evenly between OpenAI and Anthropic with a thin Gemini slice; Other Model Providers (xAI, Mistral, DeepSeek, MiniMax, Moonshot) and IaaS/Open get effectively zero AI-Native spend (their token customers skew enterprise / sovereign / hobbyist). Consumer split is engine-derived from each provider's subscription_pct rollup; Ents & Govs back-solves to satisfy each provider's customer_revenue total. Used by the engine to emit a buyer_provider_routing matrix that the renderer can surface on /revenue.html.

AI Natives.src: Editorial — cohort AI-native scale-ups consume OpenAI and Anthropic APIs roughly evenly (Cursor/Codex split, Claude Code traction, Anthropic enterprise share); Gemini ~10% per OpenRouter signals + AI Ledger source scan 2026-04-09. Other / IaaS held at 0% — AI-natives use them at the margin only.

subscription_pct.src: Subscription revenue routes 100% to Model Subs (consumer-app channel mix)

api_pct_by_archetype.frontier_lab.src: Frontier labs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) sell ~95% direct API to AI-native customers and ~5% via Bedrock/Azure OpenAI/Vertex — frontier-lab API revenue is heavily direct because customers want the latest models without resale lag.

api_pct_by_archetype.ai_native.src: AI-native scale-ups (Cursor, Glean, Harvey, Perplexity, etc.) consume frontier-lab APIs ~90% direct and ~10% via hyperscaler resale; cost and latency favour direct over resale for the AI-native cohort.

api_pct_by_archetype.enterprise_saas.src: Enterprise SaaS layering AI features (Salesforce/ServiceNow/Adobe/etc.) routes API consumption ~50% via hyperscaler resale (Azure OpenAI, Bedrock) for compliance/procurement reasons.

api_pct_by_archetype.consumer_app.src: Consumer apps don't buy through hyperscalers; 100% routes to direct API.

api_pct_by_archetype.iaas.src: IaaS aggregators are sellers of token APIs themselves; their api_pct routes 100% to Model API rather than hyperscaler resale.

api_pct_by_archetype._default.src: Default for un-tagged entities — skews to direct (85%) over hyperscaler (15%) so the channel under-counts rather than over-counts (per wq-090 edge-case rule).

enterprise_pct_by_archetype.frontier_lab.src: Frontier-lab enterprise contracts (ChatGPT Enterprise, Claude for Enterprise) route ~50% via hyperscaler partners (Azure OpenAI Service for OpenAI; AWS Bedrock for Anthropic) for procurement reasons. Direct enterprise contracts are the rest.

enterprise_pct_by_archetype.ai_native.src: AI-native scale-ups' enterprise tier routes ~20% via hyperscaler — most enterprise contracts at AI-native cos are direct because the SaaS app, not the underlying compute, is what the buyer is acquiring.

enterprise_pct_by_archetype.enterprise_s.src: Enterprise SaaS enterprise SKUs route ~60% via hyperscaler — incumbents lean on Azure / Bedrock for AI feature delivery, with the remainder coming through direct SaaS GTM.

enterprise_pct_by_archetype._default.src: Default 50/50 — symmetric prior absent archetype information. Held conservative to avoid skewing Hyperscalers over- or under-counted for un-tagged entities.

_default_for_unmapped.src: Default split for providers without revenue_by_channel populated. Skews to direct channels (Subs+API+AI Native) for new providers without enterprise/hyperscaler partnerships.

small_provider_aggregation._doc: Per brief §5. Providers below threshold_pct of total provider value collapse into 'Other Model Providers' node, with a floor of always showing top min_visible_rank by value. Provenance preserved via members[] list on the Other node.

validation_targets._doc: §4 calibration gate. Engine output for these provider slugs must land within ±15% of the hand-curated value below, OR Simon explicitly approves divergence with an override.

iaas_aggregation._doc: IaaS/Open is an aggregation node (audit §4.4: Together/Fireworks/Groq combined). Not a single entity slug. wq-055 preserves the hand-curated value for now — wq-053 tracks populating the underlying IaaS provider entities so this can become engine-derived.
iaas_aggregation.label: IaaS/Open
iaas_aggregation.src: AI Ledger methodology §7.2 ($0.5B customer + $0.3B VC). Preserved hand-curated until wq-053 populates IaaS provider entity records.

market_aggregates_estimation._doc: wq-067 — fallback parameters used when entity-level data isn't sourced. All editorial; same tiered structure as inference/opex estimation.

tokens_per_dollar_rate._doc: Estimated tokens-per-$1-revenue. Used when entity has no current.tokens_per_day AND no entity.financials.<year>.tokens_annual_inference. Per assumptions-audit.md §5.2 GPU triangulation: ~565T tokens/day total inference at ~$48B annualized provider revenue ⇒ ~4.3B tokens per dollar inference revenue. Conservative for revenue-side scaling.
tokens_per_dollar_rate.src: Derived from assumptions-audit.md §5.1 (565T tokens/day) and §4.1 ($17.47B customer revenue) annualized; rounded for stability.

capex_per_revenue_rate._doc: Used when entity has no per-source capex documented and isn't in the mag7/neocloud allocation buckets. Editorial fallback rate (capex per $1 of provider revenue).
capex_per_revenue_rate.src: Editorial allocation based on AI Ledger §4.1 totals: $250B mag7 capex (hyperscalers) ÷ ~$220B AI-attributable hyperscaler revenue ≈ 1.10. Neoclouds run roughly 2× revenue in capex due to GPU build-out. Frontier model providers spend ~30% of revenue on dedicated infra ex-cloud rentals.

capex_source_allocation._doc: Maps entity slugs to the capex source class. Used to bucket per-entity capex into mag7/neocloud/sovereign/enterprise totals. Anything not listed defaults to 'enterprise' (broad bucket for non-classified spend).
capex_source_allocation.src: Editorial classification per AI Ledger structure; sovereign list is short and growing — amend as new sovereign AI funds appear.

segment_composition_by_archetype._doc: wq-090 — per-archetype mapping from revenue_by_channel slices to the new Who-Pays buyer segments (Consumer / AI Natives / Enterprises & Govs). Replaces the wq-067 flat api_to_sme/api_to_enterprise split, which inflated SME by treating AI-native customers as if they were small businesses. subscription_pct routes 100% to Consumer for every archetype (ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, etc. are consumer-side). api_pct splits per archetype: frontier_lab and ai_native are AI-native-heavy; enterprise_saas is ents-govs-heavy. enterprise_pct routes 100% to Enterprises & Govs for all archetypes (enterprise contracts by definition).

frontier_lab.src: Frontier-lab API revenue is heavily AI-native (~70%) — Cursor / Perplexity / Glean / Harvey etc. consume the bulk of OpenAI/Anthropic API. Remaining ~30% is enterprise integrations routed via direct API.

ai_native.src: AI-native cos selling APIs themselves (Cohere on app side, Mistral) skew to ents-govs ~70% because their API customers are enterprise integrators, not other AI-natives.

enterprise_saas.src: Enterprise SaaS API revenue is 100% ents-govs by definition — nobody who isn't an enterprise is paying for Salesforce / ServiceNow API access.

consumer_app.src: Pure consumer apps rarely have meaningful api_pct revenue. 50/50 default if API revenue exists at all.

iaas.src: IaaS aggregator customer mix is hard to pin without per-customer data; held at 50/50 ai-natives vs ents-govs as neutral prior.

_default.src: Untagged entities default 40/60 ai-natives/ents-govs — slight skew to ents-govs reflects the typical mix when archetype is unknown but the entity has API revenue.

_default_split.src: Default segment split when entity has no revenue_by_channel at all. Replaces wq-067 SME-bucketed split with the new buckets.
